1. The student needs the button's area to be at least 10 square inches for all screen widths between 3 inches and 6 inches. This can be represented by the inequality:

$$
P(x) \geq 10
$$

Substituting the polynomial equation:

$$
2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 \geq 10
$$

Simplifying:

$$
2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 17 \geq 0
$$

To find the range of x-values that satisfy this inequality, we need to solve the inequality:

$$
2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 17 \geq 0
$$

This is a cubic equation, and finding the exact roots analytically is complex. Instead, we can evaluate the polynomial at the endpoints of the interval (3 and 6) to check if the inequality holds.

At $ x = 3 $:

$$
P(3) = 2(27) - 5(9) + 3(3) - 7 = 54 - 45 + 9 - 7 = 11
$$

At $ x = 6 $:

$$
P(6) = 2(216) - 5(36) + 3(6) - 7 = 432 - 180 + 18 - 7 = 263
$$

Since both values are greater than 10, the inequality $ P(x) \geq 10 $ holds for all $ x $ in the interval [3, 6].
